_comment: Project-scoped Claude Code settings for the Urfael vault. The Stop hook speaks the reply via ElevenLabs when you run `claude` directly in this vault (the overlay/daemon does its own TTS and sets URFAEL_OVERLAY=1 to suppress this).
_security: permissions.deny is a HARD boundary in Claude Code: it beats allow rules AND the permission mode (acceptEdits/bypassPermissions), and a Read() deny also covers Grep/Glob. Every claude session the daemon spawns runs in this vault, so these rules block the AGENT (heartbeat, cron, jobs, distill, even a YOLO or FULL-mode turn) from READING your credential stores AND from WRITING anywhere dangerous. The Write/Edit denies are critical: acceptEdits is not a cwd jail, so without them an injected turn could write ~/.zshrc (shell RCE), a LaunchAgent (persistence), or overwrite ~/.claude/settings.json to STRIP these very rules and then read+exfil the credentials. Denying Write to ~/.claude protects the rules themselves. The daemon still reads bridge.env via Node fs; this only constrains the model's file tools.
